---
title: "day3_reproducibility_notes"
author: "Krish"
format: html
editor: visual
toc: true
---

## R Environment Setup

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(tidyverse)
```

## Step 1: Searching for FastQC

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda search -c bioconda fastqc
```

## **Step 2: Create a new Environment**

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda create --name genomics -y  # -y means "yes" to all questions automatically
```

## **Step 3: Activating the Environment**

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda activate genomics
```

## **Step 4: Installation**

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda install -c bioconda fastqc -y
```

## **Step 5: Verification (The Help Flag)**

```{bash}
#| eval: false
fastqc -h
```

## **Micro-Challenge 4: Hello Docker**

```{bash}
#| eval: false
docker pull --platform linux/amd64 <image_name>

# Pull the BUSCO image (a common genomics tool)
# Format: docker pull <author>/<tool>:<version>
docker pull ezlabgva/busco:v6.0.0_cv1

# Breakdown of the command:
# --rm  : Automatically delete the container when the task is finished (saves space)
# -it   : Interactive Terminal (lets us see the output in real-time)
# -v    : Volume mapping. This connects your computer's folder to the container's folder.
#         Without this, the container cannot see your data!

docker run --rm -it ezlabgva/busco:v6.0.0_cv1 busco --help
```
